metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state (SQLite/PostgreSQL databases) and exposes full CRUD operations via 75+ REST API controllers with entity lifecycle management.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: Lidarr uses a single, static API key for all API access. The API key is a GUID stored in plaintext XML configuration. All authenticated requests share the same identity with no mechanism to distinguish between different callers.
findings.gap: No support for individual machine identity. All API consumers share a single identity. No principal attribution in audit logs.
findings.recommendation: Introduce per-agent API key generation with unique principal identifiers. Add a ClientId field to the authentication ticket for audit logging.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Introduce per-agent API key generation with unique principal identifiers.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Each agent identity has a unique credential that is attributed in all audit/history records and can be individually revoked.

findings.title: No Scoped Permissions
findings.description: The authorization model is binary — either you have the API key (full access to all endpoints) or you don't. No IAM policies, role definitions, or scope-based permissions exist.
findings.gap: No mechanism to grant an agent read-only access to specific resources. Every authenticated caller has identical full-admin privileges.
findings.recommendation: Implement role-based access control with at minimum a read-only role that excludes POST/PUT/DELETE operations. Support scoped API keys per consumer.

compensating_controls: Deploy an API gateway or reverse proxy that restricts agent access to specific HTTP methods and URL paths
Use a read-only database replica for agent queries

findings.title: No Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: No action-level authorization exists. Once authenticated via API key, all 75+ endpoints (read, create, update, delete) are accessible with no additional permission checks.
findings.gap: Cannot enforce that an agent may read albums but not delete them within the same resource type.
findings.recommendation: Introduce action-level authorization attributes on controllers with granular permissions.

compensating_controls: External reverse proxy with path and method-based ACLs
Network segmentation restricting agent access to specific endpoints

findings.title: No Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: Lidarr logs auth events and records entity history in the database, but logs are stored locally with no immutability guarantees and no per-caller attribution.
findings.gap: No immutable, tamper-evident audit trail. No per-caller attribution in logs or history.
findings.recommendation: Add caller identity to all EntityHistory records. Ship logs to an immutable store.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, audit logging still important for observability.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, so immutable audit is not a deployment blocker but remains a safety concern for traceability.
compensating_controls: Forward Lidarr logs to a centralized append-only log aggregation service
Implement log shipping with integrity verification

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Mechanism
findings.description: There is no mechanism to suspend an individual agent identity. The single shared API key cannot be selectively revoked — resetting it disconnects ALL consumers.
findings.gap: Cannot isolate a misbehaving agent without disrupting all API consumers.
findings.recommendation: Support multiple API keys with individual revocation.

compensating_controls: Revoke agent access at an external proxy/gateway layer
IP-based blocking at the network level

findings.title: No Inbound Rate Limiting
findings.description: No inbound rate limiting exists on any API endpoint. The RateLimitService only throttles outbound HTTP requests to external services.
findings.gap: A runaway agent loop can overwhelm the application at machine speed with no protection.
findings.recommendation: Add rate limiting middleware (e.g., AspNetCoreRateLimit) with per-key throttling.

compensating_controls: Deploy a reverse proxy with rate limiting in front of Lidarr

findings.title: No Data Residency Controls
findings.description: No data residency enforcement. Data location depends entirely on deployment. No controls preventing cross-boundary data flow to LLM endpoints.
findings.gap: No data residency configuration, no region enforcement.
findings.recommendation: Document data residency characteristics. Ensure agent architecture keeps data in-region.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, data residency is still relevant but lower risk.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents may still transmit user library data to LLM endpoints in different regions, creating residency risk.
compensating_controls: Use Amazon Bedrock in the same region as the Lidarr deployment
Restrict agent-LLM communication to in-region endpoints via network policy

findings.title: No PII Redaction in Logs
findings.description: No PII scrubbing middleware or log filtering exists. Stack traces in error responses may expose internal paths. HostConfigResource returns sensitive credentials in JSON responses.
findings.gap: No log scrubbing for sensitive data. Stack traces in error responses.
findings.recommendation: Add PII redaction to logging middleware. Remove stack traces from production error responses.

compensating_controls: Configure NLog logging level to Warning in production
Deploy a log scrubbing pipeline between Lidarr and log aggregation

findings.title: No Machine-Readable Error Codes
findings.description: Error responses include Message and Description fields with correct HTTP status codes, but no machine-readable error code or retryable indicator.
findings.gap: An agent cannot programmatically distinguish retriable from terminal errors without parsing message text.
findings.recommendation: Add an errorCode field and a retryable boolean to the error response model.

findings.title: No Breaking Change Detection in CI
findings.description: API versioned via URL path with auto-generated OpenAPI spec. Database schema versioned via FluentMigrator. No breaking change detection, no consumer-driven contract testing in CI.
findings.gap: No automated breaking change detection. Agents can be broken silently by new releases.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec diff to Azure Pipelines CI. Fail build on breaking changes.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing
findings.description: NLog with file logging and optional CLEF structured output. No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or traceparent header support. No correlation IDs.
findings.gap: Cannot trace agent-initiated requests through the system.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation with trace ID propagation.

findings.title: No Alerting Configuration
findings.description: No alerting configuration exists. Sentry captures errors but no threshold alerts configured for API error rates or latency.
findings.gap: No proactive alerting when the API degrades.
findings.recommendation: Configure alerting on API error rates and response latency.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC exists. Lidarr is deployed via platform-specific installers. No Terraform, CloudFormation, or CDK defining infrastructure.
findings.gap: No codified infrastructure for the agent-facing surface.
findings.recommendation: Create IaC for the deployment environment if Lidarr will be exposed to agents.

findings.title: No API Contract Testing in CI
findings.description: Comprehensive Azure Pipelines CI/CD with unit and integration testing, but no API contract testing or breaking change detection.
findings.gap: API changes not validated against consumer contracts.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec validation and consumer-driven contract tests to CI.

findings.title: No Automated Rollback
findings.description: Built-in self-update mechanism but no automated rollback capability. Rolling back requires manual re-installation of previous version.
findings.recommendation: Use container-based deployment with image version pinning for instant rollback.

findings.title: No Encryption at Rest
findings.description: SQLite databases stored unencrypted. PostgreSQL connections lack TLS enforcement. No KMS or encrypted storage configuration.
findings.recommendation: Enable encrypted storage at the infrastructure layer. Enforce TLS for PostgreSQL.

findings.title: Plaintext Credential Storage
findings.description: All credentials stored as plaintext in XML configuration file. API key, PostgreSQL password, SSL certificate password stored without encryption.
findings.gap: No secrets management system. No rotation mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Support external secrets providers for credential injection.

findings.title: No Staging Environment with Production Data Shape
findings.description: A devcontainer exists for development. CI uses ephemeral test environments. No documented staging environment with production-equivalent data shape for agent testing.
findings.gap: No staging environment with production-like data for agent behavior validation.
findings.recommendation: Create a Docker-based staging setup with seed data for agent testing.

findings.title: API Test Coverage Lacks Contract Testing
findings.description: Integration tests exist in src/NzbDrone.Integration.Test/ApiTests/ covering major API resources (Artist, Album, Track, DownloadClient, Indexer, Notification, Release, Command, Calendar, History, Blocklist, RootFolder, etc.) with typed REST clients. Azure Pipelines CI runs tests in a dedicated Unit_Test stage. However, there is no consumer-driven contract testing (Pact), no OpenAPI schema validation in CI, and no automated breaking-change detection for API responses.
findings.gap: No API contract testing or schema-breaking-change detection in CI. Tests validate functional behavior but do not guard against schema drift that would break agent tool bindings.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec diff validation to the CI pipeline. Consider consumer-driven contract tests (Pact) for agent-facing endpoints.

compensating_controls: Existing integration tests catch functional regressions in API behavior
Manual review of API response changes during PR review
